summary:
This is a **synthetic** findings file. It is not a real audit — it exists to exercise every component the build script renders so the `report-template.html` styles can be eyeballed after editing.

It includes all four severity tiers, both `filesDisplay` modes (`chips` and `list`), single- and multi-file issues, location-only issues, multiple code blocks, single- and multi-paragraph impact, string and list `fix` values, a custom `fixLabel`, all four recommendation phases, and a testing checklist. Inline `code` and **bold** appear throughout to confirm span rendering.

severities.label: Critical
severities.description: Broken markup or hard WCAG failures with no mitigations.

severities.label: High
severities.description: Missing ARIA patterns; fails a success criterion at **AA**.

severities.label: Medium
severities.description: Inconsistent practices that degrade the assistive-tech experience.

issues.title: Form controls have no associated `<label>`
issues.impact:
Screen-reader users hear only "edit text" with no indication of what to type. The `placeholder` disappears on focus and is **not** a substitute for a label.

Voice-control users cannot target the field by name, because it has no accessible name to speak.
fix: Add a visible `<label for>` bound to each input's `id`.
Where a visible label is undesirable, use `aria-label` or a visually-hidden label — never `placeholder` alone.
Re-run the audit to confirm every control resolves an accessible name.

issues.title: Decorative debug `<h1>` left in production template
issues.impact: Every paragraph instance announces a top-level heading, so a page with eight paragraphs has nine `<h1>`s and a meaningless heading outline.
issues.fix: Remove the debug `<h1>` line from the template and re-check the page heading outline.

issues.title: Custom dropdown is a `<div>` soup with no ARIA
issues.impact: The control is invisible to assistive tech as a listbox and cannot be operated by keyboard — no roles, no `tabindex`, no key handlers.
fix: Adopt the APG combobox or listbox pattern, or use a native `<select>`.
Confirm focus is visible and trapped appropriately while open.

issues.title: Page has no `<main>` landmark
issues.impact: Screen-reader users cannot jump to the primary content with a landmark shortcut and have no skip target.
issues.fix: Wrap the primary content region in a single `<main>` element and ensure the skip link points to it.

issues.title: Icon-only buttons rely on a `title` attribute for their name
issues.impact: `title` is inconsistently announced across screen readers and is invisible to touch users. The accessible name should come from `aria-label` instead.
issues.fix: Replace `title` with `aria-label` (or visually-hidden text) and keep `aria-hidden="true"` on the decorative `<svg>`.

issues.title: Redundant `role="button"` on a native `<button>`
issues.impact: Harmless but noisy — the implicit role is already `button`, so the attribute adds nothing and invites copy-paste of the anti-pattern.

items.text: Rebuild the custom dropdown on a known ARIA pattern.
Add the `<main>` landmark and skip target to the layout shell.
Add an axe-core check to CI to catch regressions automatically.
Sweep redundant ARIA roles from native elements.

testing.text: VoiceOver + Safari (macOS) — navigate by headings, landmarks, and form controls.
NVDA + Chrome (Windows) — verify the dropdown widget pattern.
Keyboard-only — tab through every interactive element; confirm visible focus.
axe DevTools — zero critical, zero serious violations.
Lighthouse accessibility score ≥ 90 on representative pages.
